whatThisIs: Convert between corticosteroid doses using the standard glucocorticoid-equivalence table - hydrocortisone, cortisone, prednisone, prednisolone, methylprednisolone, triamcinolone, dexamethasone, and betamethasone. Sophie Well takes the source steroid, the source dose, and the target steroid and returns the equivalent dose along with the relative anti-inflammatory and mineralocorticoid potencies and the approximate duration class (short, intermediate, long) so the converted dose is not just numerically right but pharmacodynamically sensible.
whenToUse: Use this when transitioning a patient between IV and oral steroids on admission or discharge, when calculating a taper that starts on one agent and finishes on another, or when reviewing whether a stress-dose hydrocortisone regimen matches an outpatient prednisone equivalent. The equivalence values are anti-inflammatory potency, not full pharmacokinetics - duration, mineralocorticoid activity, and HPA-axis suppression vary by agent - so the converted dose is the starting point for clinical judgment, not a substitute for it.
